response:
Here's a complete set of pytest unit tests for your `flatten_dict` function, covering all required categories:
```python
import pytest
from your_module import flatten_dict  # Update import to match your module name

# ------------------------------
# Happy Path Tests
# ------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "input_dict, parent_key, sep, expected",
    [
        # Basic multi-level nested dict
        ({"a": 1, "b": {"c": 2, "d": {"e": 3}}}, "", ".", {"a": 1, "b.c": 2, "b.d.e": 3}),
        # Already flat input dict (no nesting)
        ({"x": "hello", "y": 5, "z": True}, "", ".", {"x": "hello", "y": 5, "z": True}),
        # Custom separator (not default dot)
        ({"a": {"b": {"c": 10}}}, "", "_", {"a_b_c": 10}),
        # Custom root parent key (for manual recursive calls)
        ({"a": 1, "b": {"c": 2}}, "root", ".", {"root.a": 1, "root.b.c": 2}),
        # Mixed primitive value types
        ({"user": {"name": "Alice", "age": 30, "is_active": False}}, "", ".", 
         {"user.name": "Alice", "user.age": 30, "user.is_active": False}),
    ],
)
def test_happy_path(input_dict, parent_key, sep, expected):
    """Test valid, common use cases of the flatten function."""
    assert flatten_dict(input_dict, parent_key, sep) == expected

# ------------------------------
# Edge Case Tests
# ------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "input_dict, parent_key, sep, expected",
    [
        # Empty root input dict
        ({}, "", ".", {}),
        # Empty nested dict (contributes no keys to output)
        ({"a": {}, "b": 2}, "", ".", {"b": 2}),
        # Deeply nested empty dict
        ({"a": {"b": {"c": {}}}}, "", ".", {}),
        # Original keys already contain the separator character
        ({"a.b": {"c": 3}}, "", ".", {"a.b.c": 3}),
        # None values preserved as-is
        ({"a": None, "b": {"c": None}}, "", ".", {"a": None, "b.c": None}),
        # Non-dict iterables (lists/tuples) preserved as values
        ({"a": [1, 2, 3], "b": {"c": (4, 5)}}, "", ".", {"a": [1, 2, 3], "b.c": (4, 5)}),
        # Non-string integer keys
        ({1: {2: 3}}, "", ".", {"1.2": 3}),
        # Empty string separator
        ({"a": {"b": 1}}, "", "", {"ab": 1}),
        # Overlapping generated keys (last one wins per Python dict rules)
        ({"a": {"b": 1}, "a.b": 2}, "", ".", {"a.b": 2}),
    ],
)
def test_edge_cases(input_dict, parent_key, sep, expected):
    """Test unusual but valid inputs."""
    assert flatten_dict(input_dict, parent_key, sep) == expected

# ------------------------------
# Error Case Tests
# ------------------------------
def test_non_dict_input_raises_attribute_error():
    """Test passing non-dict inputs throw the expected error."""
    # None input
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError, match="object has no attribute 'items'"):
        flatten_dict(None)
    # List input
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError, match="object has no attribute 'items'"):
        flatten_dict([1, 2, 3])
    # String input
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError, match="object has no attribute 'items'"):
        flatten_dict("not a dict")
    # Integer input
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError, match="object has no attribute 'items'"):
        flatten_dict(123)

def test_circular_nested_dict_raises_rec
